improvements: Unified one API surface (fuzzySearch) instead of three different option shapes (match-sorter keys, fuzzysort key/keys, Fuse weighted keys).
Replaced match-sorter's remove-accents dependency with a length-preserving inline diacritics fold so highlight indices stay aligned to the original string.
Carried fuzzysort's char-bitflag early-out into the tiered ranker so non-matching items are rejected before the expensive subsequence scan.
Emit highlight output as Fuse-style [start,end] tuples via mask collapsing for every tier (not just the fuzzy tier), so callers get one consistent highlight format.
removals: Fuse's full bitap Levenshtein automaton (location/distance binary search) — heavy and not needed once a subsequence scorer covers the fuzzy tier.
fuzzysort's UMD wrapper, prepared-target caches and worker plumbing.
Fuse's WebWorker, inverted-index and token-search subsystems.
lineage: match-sorter getMatchRanking tiers (v6) → kept as primary ordering
fuzzysort v3 algorithm subsequence+indexes → adapted as MATCHES-tier fine scorer
Fuse v7 extended parseQuery + convertMaskToIndices → operator parsing + range output
merged into single zero-dep TS module

inputs.contributed: Deterministic ranking-tier ladder (CASE_SENSITIVE_EQUAL..MATCHES), acronym generation + position mapping, and the stable multi-key sort with keyIndex tie-break and localeCompare baseSort.

Subsequence fine-scorer with beginning-of-word/camelCase + contiguity bonuses for tie-breaking inside the MATCHES tier, the char-bitflag early-out for fast rejection, and the per-character match-index concept used for highlight ranges.
Extended-query operator syntax parsed into AND clauses: =exact, 'include, ^prefix, suffix$, ^...$, and ! negation.
Collapsing scattered matched character positions into contiguous [start,end] highlight ranges (indicesToRanges).

outputs.name: return
outputs.emits: ranked results: { item, refIndex, score, rank, key?, matches:[{key?,value,indices:[start,end][]}] }
